slides.title: What is RAG?
slides.content: RAG stands for Retrieval-Augmented Generation. It is an AI approach that combines information retrieval with natural language generation to provide more accurate and relevant responses.

slides.title: How RAG Works
slides.content:
- Retrieves relevant data from a knowledge base
- Generates responses based on retrieved data
- Enhances response accuracy and relevance

slides.title: Key Components of RAG
slides.content:
- **Retrieval Module:** Searches database for relevant documents.
- **Generator Module:** Utilizes retrieved information for response generation.

visual_assets.caption: Code Example of the Retrieval Concept

slides.title: Benefits of RAG
slides.content:
- Improves response accuracy
- Leverages existing knowledge
- Reduces reliance on massive datasets for training

slides.title: RAG vs Traditional Models
slides.content:
- Traditional AI relies heavily on pre-training data.
- RAG minimizes inaccuracies by using live data retrieval.

slides.title: RAG in Action
slides.content: - **Use Case:** Customer support using RAG for real-time problem-solving by retrieving and generating responses based on a comprehensive knowledge base.

visual_assets.caption: Example of RAG in a Customer Support Scenario

slides.title: Conclusion and Future of RAG
slides.content: RAG presents a promising evolution in AI, enabling more dynamic and context-aware interactions. As it evolves, the potential applications across various sectors make it a critical component in the future of AI.
